---
title: "First Blog"
author: "Zhaoshan Duan"
date: "2021-03-31"
output:
  blogdown::html_page
header-includes:
  \usepackage{emo}
categories: ["Blog"]
menu:
  sidebar:
    name: Introduction
    identifier: introduction
    weight: 10
---

This is my first blog written with `blogdown`.

This personal blog website is built with Hugo and deployed with Netlify `r emo::ji("smile")` . To write with `blogdown`, I am using [blogdown: Creating Websites with R Markdown](https://bookdown.org/yihui/blogdown/) by Yihui Xie, Amber Thomas, Alison Presmanes Hill. I find it interesting to keep track of my progress, and re-examine myself from a "third-person" point of view. I decided to create a personal blog site to serve the following purposes:

-   **Blogging** provides me with an opportunity to test out whatever I found interesting in the realms of Machine Learning and Deep Learning. I hope to explore in both R and Python. A bit of free writing may be included as well both as a mean to practice my writing skill and to have my sanity in-check `r emo::ji("cold")` .

-   **Portfolio** showcases my independent projects for potential employers. Still need to think about how to structure this page.

-   **Creative** will be populated once I gained enough confident in uploading my guitar playing. `r emo::ji("silly")`

-   Overall, the blog site aims at improving, expanding and tracking my technical skills outside my M.S. Program.

Originally, riding on the excitement of deploying the site successfully, I was planning to update the blog three times as week. Then I realize that I should stop lying to myself. So currently I am planning to write a new blog post whenever I can. I hope to explore [\#TidyTuesday](https://twitter.com/search?q=%23tidytuesday&src=typed_query) data sets and test out some code from [Tidy Modeling with R.](https://www.tmwr.org/) as well as from the personal blog website of [Julia Silge](https://juliasilge.com/) and other R giants' works. I want to experiment with Python too. However, since all of my knowledge of Python are self-taught, I will focus more on the algorithm and data structure practices.

I have decided to use as many emojis as possible in these informal blogs to compensate my seemingly emotionless writing style. The fine line between professionalism and casualness is at times hard to walk through for a Nonresident Alien `r emo::ji("alien")` like myself - an official state that was assigned to me by the United States Citizenship and Immigration Services.

> *Slow, steady progress is better than daily excuses.*
>
> \- Robin Sharma
